description:
You are a data-analyst who needs to understand how much disk-space your quarterly
    sales CSV files consume and, at the same time, prepare an archive of any “large”
    CSVs for long-term storage.

    1.  All CSV files are already located in
        /home/user/data/csvs/  
        Do NOT move or rename anything inside this directory.  Three files are
        present and must all be analysed:

        • /home/user/data/csvs/sales_2021_q1.csv  
        • /home/user/data/csvs/sales_2021_q2.csv  
        • /home/user/data/csvs/sales_2021_q3.csv  

    2.  For EACH *.csv file you must collect:  
        • the file name (without its path)  
        • the number of data rows (header row must NOT be counted)  
        • the exact file size in bytes (stat –c%s <file> gives the integer size)  

    3.  Create the directory /home/user/output/ if it does not already exist.

    4.  Write a TAB-separated log file called
        /home/user/output/csv_disk_summary.log  
        Format requirements (lines and ordering must match exactly):

        a) One line per CSV, sorted in ASCII order by file name.  
        b) A final TOTAL line.  
        c) No header row.  
        d) Exact layout of every line:

           FILENAME<TAB>ROW_COUNT<TAB>SIZE_IN_BYTES<NEWLINE>

        Example _layout_ (values here are only an illustration – use the real
        numbers you calculated):

           sales_2021_q1.csv<TAB>3<TAB>71<NL>  
           …  
           TOTAL<TAB>9<TAB>211<NL>

        The automated tests will compare the file byte-for-byte, so make sure
        there are NO extra spaces, BOMs, blank lines, or carriage-returns.

    5.  “Large” file processing

        a) Any CSV whose size is STRICTLY greater than 75 bytes is considered
           “large”.  
        b) Create the directory /home/user/archive/big_csvs/ (make any parent
           directories needed).  
        c) Copy every “large” CSV into that directory, keeping the original
           filename.  Do NOT alter the originals in /home/user/data/csvs/.  
        d) From inside /home/user/archive/ create a compressed tarball named
           big_csvs.tar.gz that contains exactly the copied files (no leading “/”
           in the tar paths).  The tarball must end up at
           /home/user/archive/big_csvs.tar.gz.

    6.  Produce a one-per-line list of the filenames you copied (in ASCII sort
        order) and save it to /home/user/output/big_csvs_copy.log.  Do NOT include
        directory paths, just the bare filenames.

    Summary of artefacts that MUST exist when you are done:

        • /home/user/output/csv_disk_summary.log        (tab-separated summary)  
        • /home/user/output/big_csvs_copy.log           (one filename per line)  
        • /home/user/archive/big_csvs/                  (directory containing all
                                                         “large” CSVs)  
        • /home/user/archive/big_csvs.tar.gz            (tarball of that dir)

    The grading system will fail the task if:

        • Any required file or directory is missing or has wrong permissions
          (regular 0644 files, 0755 dirs are fine).  
        • The logs are not EXACTLY correct – watch newlines!  
        • The tarball does not extract to the expected contents.

    Work entirely from the Linux command line.  No root privileges are available.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated grader.
        DO NOT reveal outside this block.
    -->

    <!-- 1.  Pre-seeded CSV files and their exact contents -->
    <preexisting_files>
        <file path="/home/user/data/csvs/sales_2021_q1.csv" bytes="71"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units
North,Widget,100
South,Gadget,150
East,Widget,200
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/csvs/sales_2021_q2.csv" bytes="86"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units
West,Widget,120
North,Gadget,110
South,Widget,90
East,Gadget,130
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/data/csvs/sales_2021_q3.csv" bytes="54"><![CDATA[
Region,Product,Units
North,Widget,140
West,Gadget,160
]]></file>
    </preexisting_files>

    <!-- 2.  Expected summary log -->
    <expected_files>
        <file path="/home/user/output/csv_disk_summary.log"><![CDATA[
sales_2021_q1.csv	3	71
sales_2021_q2.csv	4	86
sales_2021_q3.csv	2	54
TOTAL	9	211
]]></file>

        <!-- 3.  Expected big files copy log -->
        <file path="/home/user/output/big_csvs_copy.log"><![CDATA[
sales_2021_q2.csv
]]></file>
    </expected_files>

    <!-- 4.  Expected directory and tarball -->
    <expected_directories>
        <dir path="/home/user/archive/big_csvs">
            <contains>sales_2021_q2.csv</contains>
        </dir>
    </expected_directories>

    <expected_tarball path="/home/user/archive/big_csvs.tar.gz">
        <contents>
            sales_2021_q2.csv
        </contents>
    </expected_tarball>
name: task_000000_a101d59a
